title: Point Pairs
description:
There are $2N+1$ points on a plane. The $i$-th point is at $(X_i, Y_i)$. Two points $i$ and $j$ can be paired if $X_i = X_j$ or $Y_i = Y_j$.

For each point, determine the following:

  • If you remove this point from the set of points, you get $2N$ points. Can these $2N$ points be separated into $N$ disjoint pairs?
output: Output $2N+1$ lines. For the $i$-th line, print "OK" if all points except for the $i$-th can be separated into $N$ disjoint pairs. Otherwise print "NG".
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Summer 2016, Day 9: Grand Prix of Japan, Contest, Open Cup, 2016/2017 Season, Stage 1: Grand Prix of Japan
